name: public_health_emergency_exemption_marrowbridge_car_fair
premise: A Public Health Infrastructure response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving public health emergency exemption marrowbridge car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before markets open. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Nadia Nielsen
agents.identity: I am Nadia Nielsen, accountable for a defensible call in Public Health Infrastructure when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Elliot Patel
agents.identity: I am Elliot Patel, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Public Health Infrastructure.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before markets open. Evidence is partial. Nadia Nielsen must decide under care vs fairness. What does Nadia Nielsen do?
